summary: A brief biographical overview of Hildegard of Bingen, highlighting her role as a Benedictine abbess, visionary, and composer. The text outlines her holistic worldview, her expression of divine visions through the five senses, and her pioneering work in medieval morality plays.

principles.statement: Human beings and the natural world are deeply interconnected, requiring a holistic approach to life.
principles.rationale: She viewed the world through a wide-ranging lens that emphasized the relationship between humanity and nature, as well as mutuality between men and women.

mental_models.name: Divine Light through the Five Senses
mental_models.description: Perceiving and interpreting divine visions not merely as abstract thoughts, but as holistic sensory experiences encompassing sight, hearing, taste, smell, and touch.
mental_models.example: Hildegard explained that she saw all things in the light of God through the five senses, which she then translated into writing, music, and art.

mental_models.name: The Battle of the Human Soul
mental_models.description: A conceptualization of spiritual life as an ongoing moral and spiritual struggle within the human soul.
mental_models.example: She presented this idea as the central theme of her medieval morality play, Ordo Virtutum.
